conditions.note: Travel = in-tournament distance between match cities (great-circle; excludes the journey from home). Rest/altitude are off the real schedule; heat is a climate-normal + literature-coefficient proxy, not a 2026 forecast.

baseline.ratings_source: World-Football Elo from full international results (1872-2026), +60 Elo host advantage for Canada, Mexico, USA

cards.prediction:
# Model card — Prediction Engine

**Task.** Fast match outcome/score distribution `f(team_i, team_j)` to drive the WC2026 Monte
Carlo (win/advance/round probabilities).

**Model.** Elo (sequential, from international results) + Dixon-Coles bivariate Poisson with
additive Elo-supremacy (floored for mismatches). Knockouts: extra-time draw -> dedicated
penalty-shootout model. Optionally distilled from the Realism Engine (Phase 6).

**Evaluation.** W/D/L Brier & log-loss on a held-out international split (beats a fair base-rate
prior) and a past-tournament backtest (Euro 2024). Vectorized group sampling; exact WC2026
group tiebreakers; official fixed knockout bracket.

**Field & ratings.** The group draw is the **real 2026 draw** (recovered from the scheduled
fixtures, group letters pinned to the official draw) with a +60 Elo host bonus for
Canada/Mexico/USA; ratings are **World-Football Elo from the full international-results history
(1872-2026)**. `configs/wc2026.yaml` is only a fallback when the fixtures aren't present.

**Knockout bracket.** The knockout draw uses the **official WC2026 fixed R32 slot map** (group
position -> predetermined slot), and the eight best third-placed teams are routed by **FIFA's
published Annex-C table** (the 495-combination map), not a strength re-seed — so the simulated
crossings match the real bracket and favourites are not kept artificially apart. (A legacy
strength-reseeded bracket is retained only as the `reseed` baseline used to measure draw-luck.)

**Limitations.** Corpus Elo mixes men's/women's; the supremacy prior is scale-matched. Goalscorer
projections lack official 2026 rosters (best-available shooting-share / international-goals basis).
Not for betting.
cards.realism:
# Model card — Realism Engine

**Task.** Generate a soccer match event-by-event (events-as-language) into a believable box
score, conditioned on team strength + game state.

**Architecture.** Decoder-only transformer (~4.1M params, d_model 256, 5 layers, 8 heads), event tokenizer (vocab 413: type×outcome, coarse/shot zones, time-delta bins, team + specials). Phase 5 adds a 360 auxiliary head (pressure / nearest-defender / teammates-ahead).

**Data.** StatsBomb open data (international tournaments, full ~260-match corpus ≈547k SPADL
actions, 86% with 360), non-commercial with attribution. Leakage-aware split by competition/season.

**Training.** Teacher-forced next-token + **scheduled sampling** (E3: a curriculum that feeds the
model its own predictions late in training to cut exposure bias), M1 MPS, early stopping on val
loss. Effect (measured): the model now generates **realistic-length matches** (it no longer emits
`<END>` prematurely, so it needs far less decode-time `shot_boost` — 0.6 vs the old 1.5) at
slightly better held-out macro-F1; it is, however, *more conservative on goals*. Note: MPS kernels
are non-deterministic, so metrics wobble a few percent run-to-run — treat small deltas as noise.

**Metrics.** Per-event-type macro-F1 and calibration vs a Markov bigram baseline; distributional
realism (goals/shots/pass%/actions/duration) vs real with written tolerances (configs/eval.yaml).

**Division of labour.** The Realism Engine owns the *believable single-match box score*, not the
odds. Its conditioning is four team-strength scalars + game state (no learned team embeddings),
so distillation can only recover a strength→scoreline map that Elo + Dixon-Coles already encodes —
which is why the distilled Prediction Engine doesn't beat that baseline. Learned team embeddings
are the route to making team *identity* shift the distribution (data permitting).

**Limitations.** Small corpus -> under-generates goals at decode time (mitigated by a tuned
shot-boost; see configs/sim.yaml); match duration is still under-generated. Player identity is not
generated (scorers attributed via empirical shooter priors). Not for betting. Improve with more
data (uncap the corpus) and longer training (Colab/Kaggle T4).
